title: Java Object-Oriented Principles (OOP)
description: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) is a programming paradigm that organizes software around objects rather than functions. An object represents a real-world entity with state (data), behavior (methods), and identity.
summary: OOP organizes software around modular, reusable, secure, and maintainable objects. The four major pillars are Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism, and Abstraction.

title: Java Collections Framework
description: The Collections Framework is a unified architecture for representing and manipulating collections of objects. It contains Interfaces (List, Set, Queue, Map) and Classes (ArrayList, HashSet, PriorityQueue, HashMap).
examples:
### List vs Set vs Map
*   **List**: Ordered collection that allows duplicates (e.g. `ArrayList`, `LinkedList`).
*   **Set**: Unordered collection that does not allow duplicates (e.g. `HashSet`, `TreeSet`).
*   **Map**: Key-value pairs with unique keys (e.g. `HashMap`, `TreeMap`).

```java
// Using Stream API to filter collections
List<String> items = Arrays.asList("Apple", "Banana", "Cherry");
items.stream()
     .filter(item -> item.startsWith("B"))
     .forEach(System.out::println);
```
summary: Choose ArrayList for index-based search, LinkedList for frequent inserts/deletes, HashSet to keep unique elements, and HashMap for fast key-value lookups.

title: JVM Memory & Garbage Collection
description: The Java Virtual Machine (JVM) manages memory dynamically. JVM memory is divided into Heap, Stack, Method Area, PC Register, and Native Method Stack. Garbage Collection (GC) automatically reclaims heap memory by destroying unreachable objects.
examples:
### JVM Generations
1.  **Young Generation**: Where newly created objects are allocated. Divided into Eden space, survivor space S0, and survivor space S1. Minor GC runs here.
2.  **Old (Tenured) Generation**: Long-lived objects are moved here after surviving Young Gen thresholds. Major GC runs here.
3.  **Metaspace**: Stores class metadata (replaces PermGen in Java 8).

```java
// Suggesting JVM to run Garbage Collector (no guarantee)
System.gc();
```
summary: JVM isolates threads in stack memory while keeping objects in heap. Garbage collection cleans Eden and moves surviving objects to survivor spaces and eventually to Old Generation.

title: Spring Boot Basics & Bean Lifecycle
description: Spring Boot is an opinionated framework built on top of the Spring Framework, designed to simplify web app configuration. At its core is the IoC Container, which manages Spring Beans. The bean lifecycle includes instantiation, dependency injection, custom initialization, and destruction.
summary: Spring Boot simplifies startup configuration via auto-configuration and starter dependencies. IoC manages beans and their dependencies automatically.

title: Microservices Architecture & REST API
description: A Microservices architecture decomposes an application into small, independent, deployable services, each running in its own process and communicating via lightweight protocols (usually REST HTTP or gRPC).
summary: Microservices offer high scalability and tech stack flexibility but introduce network complexity and data consistency issues, which are managed using API Gateways and Saga patterns.
